name: Allegheny National Forest
description: The Allegheny National Forest is a National Forest in Northwestern Pennsylvania, about 100 miles northeast of Pittsburgh. The forest covers 513,175 acres of land. Within the forest is Kinzua Dam, which impounds the Allegheny River to form Allegheny Reservoir.

name: Angeles National Forest
description: The Angeles National Forest of the United States Forest Service is located in the San Gabriel Mountains and Sierra Pelona Mountains, primarily within Los Angeles County in Southern California. The forest was established in 1908 and covers 661,565 acres.

name: Angelina National Forest
description: Angelina National Forest is a United States national forest, one of four located in the piney woods region of Texas. The 153,180-acre Angelina National Forest is located in East Texas near Sam Rayburn Reservoir.

name: Apache–Sitgreaves National Forest
description: The Apache–Sitgreaves National Forests is a 2.76-million-acre United States national forest which runs along the Mogollon Rim and the White Mountains in east-central Arizona and into New Mexico.

name: Apalachicola National Forest
description: The Apalachicola National Forest is the largest U.S. National Forest in the state of Florida. It encompasses 635,019 acres and is the only national forest located in the Florida Panhandle.

name: Arapaho National Forest
description: Arapaho National Forest is a National Forest located in north-central Colorado. The forest is located in the Rocky Mountains, straddling the continental divide in the Front Range west of Denver.

name: Ashley National Forest
description: Ashley National Forest is a national forest located in northeastern Utah and southwestern Wyoming. The forest contains the Flaming Gorge National Recreation Area and the Uinta Mountains, which contains the highest mountain peak in Utah (Kings Peak).

name: Beaverhead–Deerlodge National Forest
description: The Beaverhead–Deerlodge National Forest is the largest of the National Forests in Montana. Covering 3.36 million acres, the forest stretches across eight counties in the southwestern area of the state.

name: Bienville National Forest
description: Bienville National Forest is a United States National Forest in central Mississippi, named for Jean-Baptiste Le Moyne de Bienville. It lies in parts of Scott, Smith, Jasper, and Newton counties and has an area of 178,541 acres.

name: Bighorn National Forest
description: The Bighorn National Forest is a U.S. National Forest located in northern Wyoming. At 1,105,311 acres it is one of the oldest government-protected forest lands in the U.S.

name: Bitterroot National Forest
description: Bitterroot National Forest comprises 1.587 million acres in west-central Montana and eastern Idaho. The forest is located in the Bitterroot and Sapphire Mountains with elevations ranging from 2,200 feet to 10,157 feet at Trapper Peak.

name: Black Hills National Forest
description: Black Hills National Forest is located in southwestern South Dakota and northeastern Wyoming. The forest has an area of over 1.25 million acres and includes Black Elk Peak, the tallest mountain in South Dakota.

name: Boise National Forest
description: Boise National Forest covers 2,203,703 acres of Idaho. The forest contains 9,600 miles of streams and rivers and 15,400 acres of lakes and reservoirs. It is home to 75 percent of the known populations of Sacajawea's bitterroot.

name: Bridger–Teton National Forest
description: Bridger–Teton National Forest is located in western Wyoming. The forests cover 3.4 million acres, making it the third largest National Forest outside Alaska. The forest stretches from Yellowstone National Park along the eastern boundary of Grand Teton National Park.

name: Caribou–Targhee National Forest
description: Caribou–Targhee National Forest is located in the states of Idaho and Wyoming, with a small section in Utah. The forest is broken into several separate sections and extends over 2.63 million acres. Most of the forest is a part of the Greater Yellowstone Ecosystem.

name: Carson National Forest
description: Carson National Forest is a national forest in northern New Mexico. It encompasses 6,070 square kilometers (1.5 million acres) and is administered by the United States Forest Service.

name: Chattahoochee–Oconee National Forest
description: The Chattahoochee–Oconee National Forest in northern Georgia comprises two United States National Forests. The combined total area is 867,265 acres. The forest contains several wilderness areas and major mountain peaks.

name: Chequamegon–Nicolet National Forest
description: The Chequamegon–Nicolet National Forest is a 1,530,647-acre U.S. National Forest in northern Wisconsin. The forest includes several wilderness areas and is known for its North Woods ecoregion.

name: Cherokee National Forest
description: The Cherokee National Forest is a United States National Forest located in the U.S. states of Tennessee and North Carolina. The forest was created on June 14, 1920 and encompasses an estimated area of 655,598 acres.

name: Chippewa National Forest
description: Chippewa National Forest is a national forest located in north central Minnesota, in the counties of Itasca, Cass and Beltrami. The forest was established in 1908.

name: Chugach National Forest
description: The Chugach National Forest is a 6,908,540-acre United States National Forest in south central Alaska. Covering portions of Prince William Sound, the Kenai Peninsula and the Copper River Delta, it was formed in 1907.

name: Cibola National Forest
description: The Cibola National Forest is a 1,633,783 acre United States National Forest in New Mexico. The name Cibola is thought to be the original Zuni Indian name for their pueblos or tribal lands.

name: Clearwater National Forest
description: Clearwater National Forest with headquarters on the Nez Perce Reservation at Kamiah is located in North Central Idaho. The forest is bounded on the north by the Idaho Panhandle National Forest.

name: Cleveland National Forest
description: Cleveland National Forest is a U.S. national forest in Southern California that encompasses 460,000 acres of inland montane regions. It is the southernmost U.S. National Forest of California.

name: Coconino National Forest
description: The Coconino National Forest is a 1.856-million acre United States National Forest located in northern Arizona in the vicinity of Flagstaff, with elevations ranging from 2,600 ft to 12,633 ft at Humphrey's Peak.

name: Colville National Forest
description: The Colville National Forest is a U.S. National Forest located in northeastern Washington state. The forest covers more than 950,000 acres and borders Canada.

name: Conecuh National Forest
description: The Conecuh National Forest in southern Alabama covers 83,000 acres, along the Alabama-Florida line. The forest has a distinct southern flavor of mist-laden hardwood swamps and southern coastal plain pine forest.

name: Coronado National Forest
description: The Coronado National Forest is a United States National Forest that includes an area of about 1.78 million acres spread throughout mountain ranges in southeastern Arizona and southwestern New Mexico.

name: Croatan National Forest
description: The Croatan National Forest is a U.S. national forest, established on July 29, 1936, and is located on the Atlantic coast of North Carolina. The forest is managed together with the other three North Carolina National Forests.

name: Custer National Forest
description: Custer National Forest is located primarily in the south central part of Montana but also has separate sections in northwestern South Dakota. With a total area of 1,188,130 acres, the forest comprises over 10 separate sections.

name: Daniel Boone National Forest
description: The Daniel Boone National Forest is a national forest in Kentucky. Established in 1937, it includes 708,000 acres of federally owned land. The forest is named after the explorer Daniel Boone.

name: Davy Crockett National Forest
description: Davy Crockett National Forest is in southeast Texas between the towns of Lufkin and Crockett. The forest covers a total of 160,647 acres in Houston and Trinity counties.

name: Delta National Forest
description: Delta National Forest is a U.S. National Forest in western Mississippi, located in Sharkey County. It is the smallest national forest and the only bottomland hardwood forest in the National Forest system.

name: Deschutes National Forest
description: The Deschutes National Forest is a United States national forest located in Central Oregon. The forest encompasses almost 1.6 million acres along the east side of the Cascade Range.

name: De Soto National Forest
description: De Soto National Forest, named for 16th-century Spanish conquistador Hernando de Soto, is 518,587 acres of pine forests in southern Mississippi. It is one of the most important protected areas for the biological diversity of the Gulf Coast ecoregion.

name: Dixie National Forest
description: Dixie National Forest is a United States national forest in Utah with headquarters in Cedar City. It occupies almost two million acres and stretches for about 170 miles across southern Utah.

name: Eldorado National Forest
description: Eldorado National Forest is a U.S. National Forest located in the central Sierra Nevada mountain range, in eastern California.

name: El Yunque National Forest
description: El Yunque National Forest, formerly known as the Caribbean National Forest, is a forest located in northeastern Puerto Rico. It is the only tropical rainforest in the United States National Forest System.

name: Finger Lakes National Forest
description: The Finger Lakes National Forest is a United States National Forest that encompasses 16,259 acres of Seneca and Schuyler counties, nestled between Seneca Lake and Cayuga Lake in the Finger Lakes Region.

name: Fishlake National Forest
description: Fishlake National Forest is a U.S. national forest located in south central Utah. The namesake for the forest is Fish Lake, the largest freshwater mountain lake in the state.

name: Flathead National Forest
description: The Flathead National Forest is a national forest in the western part of Montana. The forest covers 2,404,935 acres of which about 1 million acres is designated wilderness.

name: Francis Marion National Forest
description: The Francis Marion National Forest is located north of Charleston, South Carolina. It is named for revolutionary war hero Francis Marion. The forest covers 263,904 acres.

name: Fremont–Winema National Forest
description: The Fremont–Winema National Forest is a United States National Forest formed from the 2002 merger of the Fremont and Winema National Forests. They cover territory in southern Oregon.

name: Gallatin National Forest
description: The Gallatin National Forest is a United States national forest located in South-West Montana. Most of the Custer-Gallatin goes along the state's southern border.

name: George Washington and Jefferson National Forest
description: The George Washington and Jefferson National Forests is an administrative entity combining two U.S. National Forests into one of the largest areas of public land in the Eastern United States. The forests cover 1.8 million acres.

name: Gifford Pinchot National Forest
description: Gifford Pinchot National Forest is a national forest located in southern Washington. With an area of 1.32 million acres, it includes the 110,000-acre Mount St. Helens National Volcanic Monument.

name: Gila National Forest
description: The Gila National Forest is a United States national forest in New Mexico. Established in 1905, it now covers approximately 2,710,659 acres, making it the sixth largest national forest in the continental United States.

name: Grand Mesa National Forest
description: The Grand Mesa National Forest is a U.S. national forest in Western Colorado. It borders the White River National Forest to the north and the Gunnison National Forest to the east.

name: Green Mountain National Forest
description: Green Mountain National Forest is a national forest located in Vermont. Established in 1932, the forest is named for the Green Mountains which are part of the Appalachian Mountains.

name: Gunnison National Forest
description: The Gunnison National Forest is a U.S. national forest covering 1,672,136 acres in Western Colorado. The forest was created by Theodore Roosevelt on June 13, 1905 as the Cochetopa Forest Reserve.

name: Helena National Forest
description: Helena-Lewis and Clark National Forest is located in west-central Montana. Spanning 2.8-million acres, it is one of the oldest forest preserves in the U.S.

name: Hiawatha National Forest
description: Hiawatha National Forest is a 894,836-acre National Forest in the Upper Peninsula of the state of Michigan. The forest is physically divided into two subunits, commonly called the Eastside and Westside.

name: Holly Springs National Forest
description: The Holly Springs National Forest was established by the United States Forest Service on June 15, 1936. The Holly Springs Ranger District controls 155,661 acres.

name: Homochitto National Forest
description: Homochitto National Forest is a U.S. National Forest in southwestern Mississippi comprising 191,839 acres. The forest was established in 1936.

name: Hoosier National Forest
description: The Hoosier National Forest is a property managed by the United States Forest Service in the hills of southern Indiana. It has a total area of 204,303 acres.

name: Humboldt–Toiyabe National Forest
description: The Humboldt–Toiyabe National Forest is the principal U.S. National Forest in Nevada. With an area of 6,289,821 acres, it is the largest U.S. National Forest outside of Alaska.

name: Huron–Manistee National Forest
description: The Huron–Manistee National Forests comprise 978,906 acres of public lands extending across the northern lower peninsula of Michigan.

name: Idaho Panhandle National Forest
description: The Idaho Panhandle National Forests are a jointly administered set of three national forests located mostly in Idaho. The IPNF consists of more than 2.5 million acres of public lands in the panhandle of north Idaho.

name: Inyo National Forest
description: Inyo National Forest is a United States National Forest covering parts of the eastern Sierra Nevada of California and the White Mountains of California and Nevada, including Mount Whitney, the highest point in the contiguous United States.

name: Kaibab National Forest
description: Kaibab National Forest borders both the north and south rims of the Grand Canyon, in north-central Arizona. Its 1.6 million acres is divided into three sections.

name: Kisatchie National Forest
description: Kisatchie National Forest, the only National forest in Louisiana, is located in the forested piney hills and hardwood bottoms of seven central and northern parishes. It covers about 604,000 acres.

name: Klamath National Forest
description: Klamath National Forest is a 1,737,774-acre national forest, in the Klamath Mountains and Cascade Range, located in Siskiyou County in northern California.

name: Kootenai National Forest
description: The Kootenai National Forest is a national forest located in the far northwestern section of Montana and the northeasternmost lands in the Idaho panhandle. The forest covers 2.2 million acres.

name: Lassen National Forest
description: Lassen National Forest is a United States national forest of 1,700 square miles in northeastern California. It is named after pioneer Peter Lassen.

name: Lewis and Clark National Forest
description: Helena-Lewis and Clark National Forest is located in west-central Montana. Spanning 2.8-million acres, it is one of the oldest forest preserves in the U.S.

name: Lincoln National Forest
description: Lincoln National Forest is a unit of the U.S. Forest Service located in southern New Mexico. The Lincoln National Forest covers an extensive 1.1 million acres in southeastern New Mexico.

name: Lolo National Forest
description: Lolo National Forest is a national forest located in western Montana. The forest spans 2 million acres and includes four wilderness areas.

name: Los Padres National Forest
description: Los Padres National Forest is a United States national forest in southern and central California. The forest includes most of the mountainous land along the California coast from Ventura to Monterey.

name: Malheur National Forest
description: The Malheur National Forest is a National Forest in the U.S. state of Oregon. It contains more than 1.4 million acres in the Blue Mountains of eastern Oregon.

name: Manti–La Sal National Forest
description: The Manti–La Sal National Forest covers more than 1.2 million acres and is located in the central and southeastern parts of Utah. The La Sal Mountains are the second highest mountain range in Utah.

name: Mark Twain National Forest
description: Mark Twain National Forest is a U.S. National Forest located in the southern half of Missouri. It was established in 1939 and is named for author Mark Twain. The forest covers 3,068,800 acres.

name: Medicine Bow–Routt National Forest
description: Medicine Bow–Routt National Forest is a U.S. Forest Service managed area extending over 2,222,313 acres in Wyoming and Colorado. The forests were administratively combined in 1995.

name: Mendocino National Forest
description: The Mendocino National Forest is located in the Coastal Mountain Range in northwestern California and comprises 913,306 acres. It is the only national forest in California without a major paved road entering it.

name: Modoc National Forest
description: Modoc National Forest is a United States national forest in northeastern California covering 1,680,405 acres.

name: Monongahela National Forest
description: The Monongahela National Forest is a national forest located in the Allegheny Mountains of eastern West Virginia. It protects over 921,000 acres of federally managed land.

name: Mount Baker–Snoqualmie National Forest
description: The Mount Baker–Snoqualmie National Forest extends more than 140 miles along the western slopes of the Cascade Range from the Canada–US border to the northern boundary of Mount Rainier National Park.

name: Mount Hood National Forest
description: The Mount Hood National Forest is a U.S. National Forest in Oregon, located 62 miles east of Portland. The Forest extends south from the Columbia River Gorge and is named after Mount Hood, a stratovolcano and the highest mountain in the state.

name: Nantahala National Forest
description: The Nantahala National Forest is the largest of the four national forests in North Carolina. The Nantahala is the second wettest region in the Continental US and is part of the Appalachian temperate rainforest.

name: Nebraska National Forest
description: The Nebraska National Forest is a United States national forest located in western Nebraska, with a total area of 141,864 acres.

name: Nez Perce National Forest
description: The Nez Perce National Forest is a 4,000,000-acre United States National Forest located in west-central Idaho. The mountains provide wildlife habitat for timber wolf, moose, black bear, elk, and many other species.

name: Ocala National Forest
description: The Ocala National Forest is the second largest nationally protected forest in Florida. It covers 607 square miles. Established in 1908, it is the oldest national forest east of the Mississippi River.

name: Ochoco National Forest
description: The Ochoco National Forest is located in the Ochoco Mountains in Central Oregon, north and east of the city of Prineville. It encompasses 850,000 acres of rimrock, canyons, dense pine forests, and high desert terrain.

name: Okanogan National Forest
description: The Okanogan–Wenatchee National Forest is a U.S. National Forest located in Okanogan County in north-central Washington. The 1,499,013-acre forest is the second-largest national forest that is contained entirely within one county.

name: Olympic National Forest
description: Olympic National Forest is a U.S. National Forest located in Washington. With an area of 628,115 acres, it nearly surrounds Olympic National Park and the Olympic Mountain range.

name: Osceola National Forest
description: Osceola National Forest is a National Forest located in northeast Florida. It is named in honor of the noted Seminole warrior, Osceola. The forest covers approximately 200,000 acres.

name: Ottawa National Forest
description: The Ottawa National Forest is a national forest that covers 993,010 acres in the Upper Peninsula of Michigan. The forest includes much of Gogebic and Ontonagon counties.

name: Ouachita National Forest
description: The Ouachita National Forest is a vast congressionally-designated National Forest that lies in the western portion of Arkansas and portions of extreme-eastern Oklahoma.

name: Ozark–St. Francis National Forest
description: The Ozark – St. Francis National Forest is a United States National Forest located in Arkansas. It is composed of two separate forests: Ozark National Forest in the Ozark Mountains; and St. Francis National Forest on Crowley's Ridge.

name: Payette National Forest
description: The Payette National Forest is a U.S. National Forest located in central western Idaho. The land area consists of approximately 2.3 million acres of federally managed lands.

name: Pike National Forest
description: The Pike National Forest is located in the Front Range of Colorado, west of Colorado Springs including Pikes Peak. The forest encompasses 1,106,604 acres.

name: Pisgah National Forest
description: Pisgah National Forest is a National Forest in the Appalachian Mountains of western North Carolina. It is administered by the United States Forest Service. The forest encompasses over 500,000 acres.

name: Plumas National Forest
description: Plumas National Forest is a 1,146,000-acre United States national forest located in northern California at the northern terminus of the Sierra Nevada mountain range.

name: Prescott National Forest
description: The Prescott National Forest is a 1.25 million-acre United States National Forest located in north central Arizona in the vicinity of Prescott.

name: Rio Grande National Forest
description: Rio Grande National Forest is a 1.86 million-acre U.S. national forest located in southwestern Colorado. The Rio Grande rises in the forest, and the Continental Divide runs along most of its western border.

name: Rogue River–Siskiyou National Forest
description: The Rogue River–Siskiyou National Forest ranges from the crest of the Cascade Range west into the Siskiyou Mountains, covering almost 1.8 million acres.

name: Roosevelt National Forest
description: The Roosevelt National Forest is a national forest located in north central Colorado. It is contiguous with the Colorado State Forest as well as the Arapaho National Forest and the Routt National Forest.

name: Sabine National Forest
description: Sabine National Forest is located in East Texas near the Texas-Louisiana border. The forest covers a total of 160,873 acres and is notable for extensive forests of American beech and other hardwood trees.

name: Salmon–Challis National Forest
description: Salmon–Challis National Forest is located in east central sections of Idaho. At 4,235,940 acres it is one of the largest national forests in the lower 48 states.

name: Sam Houston National Forest
description: The Sam Houston National Forest, one of four national forests in Texas, is located 50 miles north of Houston. The forest covers approximately 163,000 acres.

name: Samuel R. McKelvie National Forest
description: The Samuel R. McKelvie National Forest is a United States national forest in the north-central Sandhills region of Nebraska. The national forest covers 116,079 acres.

name: San Bernardino National Forest
description: The San Bernardino National Forest is a United States national forest in Southern California encompassing 823,816 acres. The forest includes seven wilderness areas.

name: San Isabel National Forest
description: San Isabel National Forest is located in central Colorado. The forest contains 19 of the state's 53 fourteeners, peaks over 14,000 feet high, including Mount Elbert, the highest point in Colorado.

name: San Juan National Forest
description: The San Juan National Forest is a U.S. national forest covering over 1,878,846 acres in western Colorado. The forest covers most of the southern portion of the San Juan Mountains west of the Continental Divide.

name: Santa Fe National Forest
description: The Santa Fe National Forest is a protected national forest in northern New Mexico. It was established in 1915 and covers 1,558,452 acres.

name: Sawtooth National Forest
description: Sawtooth National Forest covers 2,110,408 acres in Idaho and Utah. Established in 1905, it includes the Sawtooth National Recreation Area and several wilderness areas.

name: Sequoia National Forest
description: Sequoia National Forest is a U.S. national forest located in the southern Sierra Nevada mountains of California. It is named for the majestic Giant Sequoia trees which populate 38 distinct groves within its boundaries.

name: Shasta–Trinity National Forest
description: The Shasta–Trinity National Forest is a federally designated forest in northern California. It is the largest National Forest in California and is managed by the U.S. Forest Service. The 2,210,485 acre forest encompasses five wilderness areas.

name: Shawnee National Forest
description: The Shawnee National Forest is a United States National Forest located in the Shawnee Hills of Southern Illinois. It consists of approximately 498,615 acres of federally managed lands.

name: Shoshone National Forest
description: Shoshone National Forest is the first federally protected National Forest in the United States and covers nearly 2,500,000 acres in Wyoming. It is part of the Greater Yellowstone Ecosystem.

name: Sierra National Forest
description: Sierra National Forest is a U.S. national forest located on the western slope of the central Sierra Nevada in California. The forest is known for its mountain scenery and beautiful lakes.

name: Siuslaw National Forest
description: The Siuslaw National Forest is a national forest in western Oregon. Established in 1908, the Siuslaw is made up of a wide variety of ecosystems, ranging from coastal forests to sand dunes.

name: Six Rivers National Forest
description: The Six Rivers National Forest is a U.S. National Forest located in the northwestern corner of California, covering approximately 1 million acres.

name: Stanislaus National Forest
description: Stanislaus National Forest is a U.S. national forest which manages 898,099 acres of land in four counties in the Sierra Nevada in Northern California. It was established on February 22, 1897.

name: Sumter National Forest
description: The Sumter National Forest is one of two forests in South Carolina managed by the United States Forest Service. The Sumter National Forest consists of 370,442 acres divided into 3 distinct sections.

name: Superior National Forest
description: The Superior National Forest is located in the Arrowhead Region of Minnesota. The forest comprises over 3,900,000 acres of woods and waters. Slightly over a quarter of the forest is set aside as the Boundary Waters Canoe Area.

name: Tahoe National Forest
description: Tahoe National Forest is a United States National Forest located in California, northwest of Lake Tahoe. It includes the 8,587-foot peak of Sierra Buttes. The forest covers 871,495 acres.

name: Talladega National Forest
description: The Talladega National Forest is located in Alabama and covers 392,567 acres at the southern edge of the Appalachian Mountains. The forest is home to several threatened and endangered species.

name: Tombigbee National Forest
description: Tombigbee National Forest is a U.S. National Forest in eastern and northeastern Mississippi. It is divided geographically into two non-contiguous sections with a total area of 67,005 acres.

name: Tongass National Forest
description: The Tongass National Forest in Southeast Alaska is the largest U.S. National Forest at 16.7 million acres. Most of its area is temperate rain forest and is remote enough to be home to many species of endangered and rare flora and fauna.

name: Tonto National Forest
description: The Tonto National Forest, encompassing 2,873,200 acres, is the largest of the six national forests in Arizona. The forest has diverse scenery, with elevations ranging from 1,400 feet in the Sonoran Desert to 7,400 feet.

name: Tuskegee National Forest
description: The Tuskegee National Forest is a U.S. National Forest located in Macon County, Alabama, just north of Tuskegee. It is the smallest national forest in the U.S. at only 11,349 acres.

name: Uinta–Wasatch–Cache National Forest
description: Wasatch–Cache National Forest is a United States national forest located primarily in northern Utah. The forest boundaries include 1,607,177 acres of land. With the newly included Uinta National Forest, the forest expands to 2,487,896 acres.

name: Umatilla National Forest
description: The Umatilla National Forest, in the Blue Mountains of northeast Oregon and southeast Washington, covers an area of 1.4 million acres.

name: Umpqua National Forest
description: Umpqua National Forest, in southern Oregon's Cascade Range, covers an area of 983,129 acres and borders the Crater Lake National Park in Southern Oregon.

name: Uncompahgre National Forest
description: Uncompahgre National Forest is a U.S. National Forest covering 955,229 acres in western Colorado. The forest contains three alpine wilderness areas.

name: Uwharrie National Forest
description: Uwharrie National Forest is a federally designated national forest region located primarily in Montgomery County, but also extending into Randolph and Davidson counties in south central North Carolina with a total area of 50,645 acres.

name: Wallowa-Whitman National Forest
description: The Wallowa–Whitman National Forest is a United States National Forest in the U.S. states of Oregon and Idaho. Formed upon the merger of the Wallowa and Whitman national forests in 1954, it covers over 2.2 million acres.

name: Wayne National Forest
description: The Wayne National Forest is located in the Appalachian part of Ohio. It is the first and only national forest in Ohio, covering 243,180 acres.

name: White Mountain National Forest
description: The White Mountain National Forest was established in 1918. It has a total area of 750,852 acres. Most of the WMNF is in New Hampshire; a small part is in neighboring Maine.

name: White River National Forest
description: White River National Forest is a National Forest in northwest Colorado. It is the most visited National Forest in the United States, primarily from users of the twelve ski areas within its boundaries. The forest contains 2,285,970 acres.

name: Willamette National Forest
description: The Willamette National Forest is a National Forest located in the central portion of the Cascade Range of Oregon. It comprises 1,678,031 acres. Over 380,000 acres are designated wilderness.

name: William B. Bankhead National Forest
description: The William B. Bankhead National Forest is one of Alabama's four National Forests, covering 181,230 acres. Known as the 'land of a thousand waterfalls', this forest is popular for outdoor recreation. Within the forest lies the Sipsey Wilderness.
